summary: The E46 is generally reliable for a BMW of its era, but cooling system failures and subframe cracks are well-documented issues that require proactive maintenance.

issues.name: Cooling System Failure
issues.description: The expansion tank, water pump, and thermostat are known to fail, often without warning. Overheating can cause head gasket damage.

issues.name: Rear Subframe Cracks
issues.description: The rear subframe mounting points can crack through the floor pan, especially on cars driven hard or in areas with road salt. A known E46 weakness.

issues.name: VANOS System Wear
issues.description: The VANOS variable valve timing system develops worn seals and rattling solenoids, causing rough idle, loss of low-end torque, and poor fuel economy.

issues.name: Window Regulator Failure
issues.description: The power window regulators are fragile and commonly fail, causing windows to drop into the door or stop working entirely.

issues.name: Control Arm Bushings
issues.description: Front control arm bushings wear out and cause clunking over bumps and vague steering feel. The entire front suspension geometry suffers.

buyingTips: Check for subframe cracks — look at the rear subframe mounting points for any signs of cracking or repair
Inspect the cooling system — if original, budget $800–$1,200 for a complete refresh including expansion tank, water pump, thermostat, and hoses
Listen for VANOS rattle on cold start — a ticking or rattling noise from the front of the engine indicates worn VANOS seals
Check service history for timing chain guides — the M54 engine uses a chain but the guides can wear and break
Test all four power windows — regulator failure is extremely common and a good negotiation point
